zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Beauchamp於1298年出生在英國瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素時，她的父親Walter de Beauchamp11295歲，母親Alice de Tosny45歲。她和Sir Robert de Lisle of Biggleswade, 1st Baron de Lisle至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1339年在英國貝德福爾比格萊斯瓦德享年41歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Beauchamp于1298年出生在英国瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素时，她的父亲Walter de Beauchamp11295岁，母亲Alice de Tosny45岁。她和Sir Robert de Lisle of Biggleswade, 1st Baron de Lisle至少有3个儿子和3个女儿。她去世于1339年在英国貝德福爾比格萊斯瓦德享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Beauchamp was born in 1298, in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, her father, Walter de Beauchamp, was 11295 and her mother, Alice de Tosny, was 45. She had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Sir Robert de Lisle of Biggleswade, 1st Baron de Lisle. She died in 1339, in Biggleswade, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Beauchamp est née en 1298, à Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Walter de Beauchamp, avait 11295 ans et sa mère, Alice de Tosny, avait 45 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Sir Robert de Lisle of Biggleswade, 1st Baron de Lisle. Elle est décédée en 1339, à Biggleswade, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Beauchamp 1298 in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter de Beauchamp 11295 und ihre Mutter Alice de Tosny 45. Sie hatte mit Sir Robert de Lisle of Biggleswade, 1st Baron de Lisle mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1339 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Biggleswade, Bedfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Beauchamp è nata il nel 1298 nel Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, sua padre, Walter de Beauchamp, aveva 11295 anni e sua madre, Alice de Tosny, aveva 45 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie con Sir Robert de Lisle of Biggleswade, 1st Baron de Lisle. Morì nel 1339, nel Biggleswade, Bedfordshire, England, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Beauchampが1298年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックワーウィック カストルで生まれた時，父Walter de Beauchampは11295歳，母Alice de Tosnyは45歳であった。彼女はSir Robert de Lisle of Biggleswade, 1st Baron de Lisleとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1339年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアビググレスワドで41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Beauchamp(이)가 1298년 영국 워윅실르 워윅 워윅 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter de Beauchamp(은)는 11295세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Tosny(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 Sir Robert de Lisle of Biggleswade, 1st Baron de Lisle(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1339년 영국 베드퍼드실르 비글레스웨이드에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Beauchamp nasceu em 1298, em Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Walter de Beauchamp, tinha 11295 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Tosny, tinha 45 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas com Sir Robert de Lisle of Biggleswade, 1st Baron de Lisle. Ela faleceu em 1339, em Biggleswade, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Beauchamp родилась в 1298 г. в местности варвикк кастл, варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Walter de Beauchamp было 11295 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Tosny, 45. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Sir Robert de Lisle of Biggleswade, 1st Baron de Lisle. Она умерла в 1339 г. в местности биглесвад, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Beauchamp nació en 1298, en Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Walter de Beauchamp, tenía 11295 años y su mamá, Alice de Tosny, tenía 45 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas con Sir Robert de Lisle of Biggleswade, 1st Baron de Lisle. Murió en 1339, en Biggleswade, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 41 años.
